growth_records.cite: Martin et al. A physiological perspective on the origin and evolution of photosynthesis. FEMS Microbiology Reviews (2017). DOI:10.1093/femsre/fux056. [PMC5972617]
growth_records.snippet: A stable population of a photosynthetic GSB, Prosthecochloris phaeobacteroides BS1, lives there at a depth of ∼100 to 110 m and has an estimated in situ doubling time of ≥2.8 years (Overmann and Pfennig 1992) or more
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (≥2.8 years = 2.8*365*24 ≈ 24,528 h) is stated explicitly in this review's text but is a secondary citation of Overmann and Pfennig (1992), not primary data generated by this paper. Reflects an in situ natural chemocline population (Black Sea), not a defined lab medium/culture — no growth_rate_per_h computed because the doubling time is a lower-bound estimate ("≥"), so mu=ln2/td would misrepresent it as exact; recorded as doubling_time_h only. Confidence low given secondary-source origin and open-ended (≥) value.
